title: Schoenoplectus fohaiensis
taxonomy: Schoenoplectus fohaiensis is classified within the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as the sedge family. This classification places it in the order Poales and the class Liliopsida. As a member of the sedge family, Schoenoplectus fohaiensis is related to other plants such as rushes and sedges. The genus Schoenoplectus is itself part of the tribe Schoeneae and the subfamily Cyperoideae. Further classification at the species level may involve more specific characteristics and genetic analysis.

intro: Schoenoplectus fohaiensis, also known as Fohai cattail, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). Research has shown that Schoenoplectus fohaiensis possesses various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to protect against oxidative stress, inflammation, and infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Schoenoplectus fohaiensis is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
